pi_items.metadata_value: Multi-window CT quality survey for systematic pathology review
pi_items.verified_value: Multi-window CT quality survey for systematic pathology review
pi_items.source: Matches the task description's prompt for the agent to perform a multi-window CT quality survey.

Verified as standard medical imaging Hounsfield Unit (HU) window levels for these tissues. export_result.sh evaluates the report for the keywords 'lung', 'bone', 'soft tissue', 'mediastin', and W/L numeric patterns.

export_result.sh and verifier.py rigorously check for 4 PNG exports > 5KB, a report > 100 bytes containing 4+ distinct numerical measurements in 5-300mm range, and window name mentions or W/L format matches.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires the agent to role-play as a radiologic technologist and perform a 4-window CT review. The input data is downloaded from Rubo Medical (dicom_viewer_0002.zip). Crucially, while the task specifies a CT scan scenario with standard Hounsfield Unit (HU) window presets (Lung W:1500/L:-500, Bone W:2000/L:300, etc.), the actual downloaded DICOM file (0002.DCM) is an 8-bit multi-frame X-Ray Angiography (Modality XA) sequence with pixel values strictly ranging from 0 to 255 and no RescaleIntercept/Slope. Applying standard CT window levels to this image is visually nonsensical. The verifier solely evaluates the presence of 4+ PNG exports (>5KB each) and a text report (>100 bytes) containing at least 4 distinct numerical measurements (between 5 and 300) and window name mentions (lung, bone, soft tissue, mediastin) or W/L numeric patterns; it does not validate the image content or correctness of annotations.
